summary: Excellent matrix capture of a classic 1971 show highlighted by an electrifying "Good Lovin'" and strong Pigpen vocals.
review: Fans rave about the seamless, un‑edited flow of this 1971 performance, especially the blistering rendition of “Good Lovin’” where Pigpen’s organ and vocals shine. The matrix source preserves crowd ambience and delivers a clean, well‑balanced sound that lets the band’s early‑year energy come through clearly.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are overwhelmingly positive about both the musical performance and the matrix recording quality, with no substantive criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crowd not edited out between songs; clear mix with prominent Pigpen organ/vocals. No major audio flaws reported, only complaints about post‑release track splitting.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers love the continuous flow with no pauses; early‑1971 boogie vibe and strong band interplay are repeatedly praised.

band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen is noted as solid/ON, delivering top‑form organ work and vocals.
